title: Otherwise

jokes.text: Good luck, Dwight.
jokes.setup: Host wishes contestant good luck right before announcing the nominees
jokes.punchline: The timing implies Dwight needs luck because he's about to lose
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic timing joke - economical but predictable

jokes.text: Don't feel bad, son. We couldn't be more proud of you. Unless, of course, you had won the award.
jokes.setup: Father consoling son with seemingly supportive words
jokes.punchline: Immediate undercut revealing conditional pride
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of parental consolation trope with perfect timing

jokes.text: Mike Pisarik, lion.
jokes.setup: Memorial for delivery boys who died on the job, listing normal causes
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and random cause of death
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation in the death cause list - economical and darkly funny

jokes.text: They're on the truck to Heaven now.
jokes.setup: Memorial for dead delivery boys
jokes.punchline: Delivery-themed euphemism for death
jokes.explanation: Thematically integrated pun that fits the delivery boy context well

jokes.text: And the fish sticks were limp!
jokes.setup: Fry's emotional rant about Professor Farnsworth being heartless
jokes.punchline: Random complaint about food quality amid serious accusations
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry non-sequitur that deflates dramatic moment perfectly

jokes.text: Ooh. Hefty. You could really bash in a skull with this thing.
jokes.setup: Fry receiving his award trophy
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinks of violent use for award
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - inappropriate dark thought at ceremony

jokes.text: I came down with a searing case of 'who-gives-a-crap?'
jokes.setup: Professor explaining why he missed Fry's award ceremony
jokes.punchline: Treating apathy as a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor dismissive humor with medical terminology twist

jokes.text: Sure, they exhibit all the telltale signs. Toenails growing, hearts pumping fluid, the whole shmagoigle.
jokes.setup: Asked if Professor's parents are still alive
jokes.punchline: Lists basic life functions as 'telltale signs' with made-up word 'shmagoigle'
jokes.explanation: Good absurd understatement with invented word that sounds perfectly Bender-esque

jokes.text: So where do these fossils live? In sedimentary rock?
jokes.setup: Asking where the Professor's elderly parents live
jokes.punchline: Fossil/rock geological pun about old people
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed geological pun with perfect setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: they live in a virtual retirement home on the Near-Death Star
jokes.setup: Explaining where the elderly parents live
jokes.punchline: Death Star pun combined with retirement home concept
jokes.explanation: Star Wars reference with death/elderly theme - solid sci-fi comedy

jokes.text: Oh, really? Does that rule apply to Clippie winners?
jokes.setup: Guard says visitors are forbidden
jokes.punchline: Fry acts like delivery award gives him special privileges
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's inflated sense of importance

jokes.text: My God! It's full of geezers.
jokes.setup: Entering the retirement facility
jokes.punchline: 2001: A Space Odyssey reference adapted for old people
jokes.explanation: Classic movie reference with perfect context substitution

jokes.text: My God! It's full of geezers.
jokes.setup: 2001: A Space Odyssey reference setup
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'stars' with 'geezers' for retirement home
jokes.explanation: Classic movie reference with perfect word substitution, economical and effective

jokes.text: Is it some kind of Craft-matic adjustable death bed?
jokes.setup: Seeing elderly people hooked up to machines
jokes.punchline: TV commercial product name twisted for morbid purpose
jokes.explanation: Clever commercial reference with dark comedy twist

jokes.text: Like a potato? Or a battery?
jokes.setup: Questioning the logic of using humans as batteries
jokes.punchline: Suggesting obviously better alternatives, including the thing they're replacing
jokes.explanation: Logical Leela pointing out obvious plot holes with perfect economy

jokes.text: it seemed like the single crummiest, laziest, most awful, dimwitted idea in the entire history of science fiction, but it turned out to be true. Who knew?
jokes.setup: Explaining The Matrix's human battery concept
jokes.punchline: Extremely harsh critique followed by casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on sci-fi tropes and movie criticism

jokes.text: Good work, writer of The Matrix.
jokes.setup: Just established Matrix was correct about human batteries
jokes.punchline: Casual congratulations to actual filmmaker
jokes.explanation: Nice capper to the Matrix sequence with fourth-wall-breaking congratulations

jokes.text: I'm gonna call them Gram-gram and Shabba-doo.
jokes.setup: Fry seeing his elderly relatives for first time
jokes.punchline: Childish nicknames including random dance reference
jokes.explanation: Pure Fry character moment with absurd nickname choices

jokes.text: They look just like you, Fry. Arms, legs, ugly.
jokes.setup: Observing family resemblance
jokes.punchline: Lists normal features then insults
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender insult structure with good misdirection

jokes.text: It's not just safe, it's 40% safe.
jokes.setup: Leela asking if virtual reality helmets are safe
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature percentage joke applied to safety
jokes.explanation: Familiar Bender callback that works in context despite being formulaic

jokes.text: There's only one word for it. Terrible. And also horrible.
jokes.setup: Describing the virtual retirement home
jokes.punchline: Claims one word then gives two
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke that fits Fry's character

jokes.text: Forget it, you flimflammer. I can smell a scam a mile away.
jokes.setup: Fry introducing himself as relative
jokes.punchline: Elderly man suspicious of genuine family member
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly paranoia joke with period-appropriate language

jokes.text: Reverse mortgages. Get your reverse mortgages here.
jokes.setup: Scene in virtual retirement home
jokes.punchline: Someone hawking predatory financial products to elderly residents
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on financial exploitation of elderly. Good background detail.

jokes.text: Reverse mortgages. Get your reverse mortgages here.
jokes.setup: Scene in retirement home
jokes.punchline: Financial scam being hawked like carnival food
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about predatory financial practices targeting elderly

jokes.text: See, I got frozen, and your son, Professor Farnsworth, is my great-great-great- great-great-great... Eh? What? Yep, I'm your grandson all right.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to explain complex family relationship
jokes.punchline: Gives up on complicated explanation and simplifies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's impatience with complexity

jokes.text: Most recently, yes.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's in high school
jokes.punchline: Technically accurate answer about his frozen timeline
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and perfect Fry literal-mindedness about time travel

jokes.text: I don't cook much since we moved out of reality.
jokes.setup: Discussing her cooking
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions living in virtual reality as cooking excuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant casual delivery of existential concept, perfect economy

jokes.text: That's a doozy. Hey, look at this. I can pull my thumb off.
jokes.setup: Responding to grandfather's injury stories
jokes.punchline: Matches serious injuries with simple magic trick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry social obliviousness - thinks thumb trick compares to real injuries

jokes.text: Stop, Shabba-doo, you're freaking me out.
jokes.setup: Ned doing thumb trick
jokes.punchline: Fry using the silly nickname in genuine distress
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier nickname joke with emotional contrast

jokes.text: You should have seen him when he was little. My golly, he was crotchety and moldy.
jokes.setup: Fry describes Professor as crotchety and moldy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was the same way as a child
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that Professor was always this way

jokes.text: rigged Bingo!
jokes.setup: Describing entertainment at retirement home
jokes.punchline: Even bingo is corrupt in this place
jokes.explanation: Economical joke about institutional corruption

jokes.text: What? Oh, God, I'm still here?
jokes.setup: Grandparents asking him to keep up with studies
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's been unconsciously responding and is horrified
jokes.explanation: Good moment of Fry being caught in automatic social response

jokes.text: I don't think Gram-gram likes that place. And I know Shabba-don't.
jokes.setup: Observing grandparents don't like retirement home
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Shabba-doo' nickname
jokes.explanation: Decent pun callback though somewhat forced

jokes.text: Oh, I'm about to lose history's greatest life all because of some useless old people.
jokes.setup: Ship won't start, putting them in danger
jokes.punchline: Bender's selfish perspective on elderly people's worth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness in crisis situation

jokes.text: They're not useless. They can cook ham casserole, and watch TV at incredible volume, and they generate electricity.
jokes.setup: Defending elderly people's usefulness
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd/mechanical skills ending with literal function
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from stereotypical elderly traits to literal battery function

jokes.text: I meant Feingold!
jokes.setup: Giving directions using elderly people as landmarks
jokes.punchline: Correcting himself on random old person's name
jokes.explanation: Absurd concern for accuracy when using people as directions

jokes.text: Hey, Zoidberg, you forgot to empty this trash can.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg introducing himself as important
jokes.punchline: Immediately reminded of menial duties
jokes.explanation: Standard Zoidberg status deflation joke

jokes.text: Are they my zombies from Hammacher Schlemmer?
jokes.setup: Told special old people are here for him
jokes.punchline: Assumes they're mail-order zombies from catalog company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Professor moment - treating zombie catalog orders as normal

jokes.text: Are they my zombies from Hammacher Schlemmer?
jokes.setup: Told special old people are here for him
jokes.punchline: Expects mail-order zombies from upscale catalog
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd juxtaposition of zombies with upscale shopping catalog

jokes.text: What crawled up his Geritol?
jokes.setup: Professor rudely rejects his parents
jokes.punchline: Elderly-specific version of common expression
jokes.explanation: Clever age-appropriate adaptation of 'crawled up his ass' - good character integration

jokes.text: Lady, all of human history happened in your past!
jokes.setup: Velma mentioning troubling things in their past
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious scope of elderly people's lifetime
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about extreme age - perfectly delivered

jokes.text: "Live it up"? I'll be surprised if you live it out!
jokes.setup: Elderly people want to go out and live it up
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on mortality and having fun
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay with dark comedy edge about elderly mortality

jokes.text: 'Live it up'? I'll be surprised if you live it out!
jokes.setup: Grandparents want to 'live it up'
jokes.punchline: Pun on their mortality prospects
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay with dark humor, excellent economy and classic Bender insensitivity

jokes.text: Gram-gram fell down at the ice cream store and we got free ice cream!
jokes.setup: Describing fun day out with elderly relatives
jokes.punchline: Treating injury as benefit for free treats
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness to inappropriate silver lining

jokes.text: I just want my parents to love me. God, how I hate them!
jokes.setup: Professor expressing vulnerability about parents
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with hatred
jokes.explanation: Good character psychology - simultaneous love and hate, perfect Professor emotional confusion

jokes.text: I just want my parents to love me. God, how I hate them!
jokes.setup: Professor expressing emotional vulnerability
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction revealing true feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contradiction showing Professor's emotional complexity

jokes.text: They never played with me like that. They were always 'too tired.' Oh, what awful parents!
jokes.setup: Jealous of Fry's relationship with his parents
jokes.punchline: Condemns parents for being tired while caring for brother
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later revelation about why parents were tired

jokes.text: But my parents were simple hedge fund managers who couldn't appreciate my interest in science.
jokes.setup: Describing his parents' background
jokes.punchline: Calls hedge fund managers 'simple' as if it's humble work
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on perspective - hedge fund managing as 'simple' work

jokes.text: Honey, I'm afraid we can't let you go. You're just not emotionally mature enough for college yet.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they won't let young Hubert go to MIT
jokes.punchline: Said to clearly adult-looking 'teenager'
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Professor's appearance even as a 'child'

jokes.text: 'Not mature enough...'
jokes.setup: Parents refuse to let genius child go to college
jokes.punchline: Visual shows him as clearly more mature than his age
jokes.explanation: Basic irony about child genius being called immature

jokes.text: Oh, I hate you, I hate you, I hate you! You ruined my life! Goodbye!
jokes.setup: Leela suggests honest communication with parents
jokes.punchline: Professor's version of 'honest' is purely hostile
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor interpretation of advice - technically honest but completely wrong

jokes.text: That is one crazy, uncircumcised old man.
jokes.setup: Watching naked Professor run away
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific anatomical observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender inappropriate observation with unexpected detail

jokes.text: but the wind caught his skin flaps and sent him parasailing over the Queensboro Bridge.
jokes.setup: Trying to intercept Professor
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of elderly man's loose skin acting as sail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd visual - aging body's weakness becomes accidental transportation method

jokes.text: Why would he go to Queens? He doesn't need tires.
jokes.setup: Wondering why Professor went to Queens
jokes.punchline: Queens stereotype about tire shops
jokes.explanation: Regional stereotype joke with logical robot perspective

jokes.text: Put on your glasses, sweetie.
jokes.setup: Looking at old farm
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman needs glasses to see destruction
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly vision joke with visual payoff

jokes.text: He was our crazy-ass nut job, and we loved him.
jokes.setup: Continuing story about troubled older son
jokes.punchline: Adopts husband's crude term as term of endearment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that transforms insult into expression of parental love

jokes.text: Yeah, sweat. Right.
jokes.setup: Velma mentioning brother's sweaty pajamas from night terrors
jokes.punchline: Implying it wasn't actually sweat
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo joke that doesn't add much

jokes.text: He was admitted to a prestigious institution. A mental institution!
jokes.setup: Build up of 'prestigious institution'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's psychiatric facility
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with strong structure, classic setup-punchline

jokes.text: Got himself a full wackademic scholarship.
jokes.setup: Talking about brother getting into institution
jokes.punchline: Academic/wacky portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay mixing academic and crazy

jokes.text: online doctorate in Rodeo Studies
jokes.setup: Explaining Floyd's education
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous degree program
jokes.explanation: Absurd degree that explains Floyd's career path

jokes.text: Floyd? Who the hell's Floyd? I'm Hubert!
jokes.setup: Parents have been talking to wrong son all along
jokes.punchline: Major identity revelation
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist that recontextualizes entire backstory - well-earned surprise

jokes.text: It felt like a minute compared to grad school.
jokes.setup: Professor was in mental institution for 25 years
jokes.punchline: Comparison suggests grad school is worse than psychiatric confinement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant academic humor with perfect Professor perspective - mental institution preferable to grad school

jokes.text: a homeless rodeo clown named Floyd came to the door claiming he was...
jokes.setup: Starting to explain about Floyd
jokes.punchline: Confirms the other brother exists and matches the description
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ties together the two sons' paths

jokes.text: My damn skin even hurts.
jokes.setup: Listing physical complaints from their adventure
jokes.punchline: Escalates to skin itself causing pain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of elderly complaints to absurd level

jokes.text: Appearances. Just a simple matter of appearances.
jokes.setup: Parents amazed by beautiful virtual recreation of farm
jokes.punchline: Professor dismissively reduces complex virtual reality programming to mere appearances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor modesty about his incredible technical achievement

jokes.text: Appearances. Just a simple matter of appearances.
jokes.setup: Parents commenting on beautiful virtual farm
jokes.punchline: Professor's dismissive scientific response to emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate deflection of sentiment with technical accuracy

jokes.text: Not too tired to chase you, you cute little nut job!
jokes.setup: Finally getting to play with his son after learning the truth
jokes.punchline: Combines affectionate play with his crude terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect resolution callback combining father's crude language with finally available parental energy

tmdb_overview: When Fry proposes to Leela, he is overcome by a near-fatal attack of déjà vu.
